lines.speaker: Dutch Phrases by
lines.text: Kenny Walgraef
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is sitting in the living room as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.
Monica notices something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! You cleaned! (Gasps) Look at these floors! You did the windows! Oh, I have been begging you for months and you did! You cleaned! And nagging works!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know uh, I didn't actually do this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no, was I cleaning in my sleep again?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, it wasn't you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well then who?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I got a maid. Yay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (shocked) I hope by maid you mean mistress, because if some other woman was here cleaning then...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh honey, I know you don't like to relinquish control...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, relinquish is just a fancy word for lose!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, she's really nice. Okay? And she mentioned that she adored the way that you arranged the sponges.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did she really say that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, I distinctly remember 'cause I thought it was a joke. Now just give her a chance, okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine, I can do it. (Gets anxious.) Whew.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, usually when I'm this anxious, I clean!
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Ross, Rachel, and Phoebe are there as someone's cell phone starts to ring with one of those fancy ring tones.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looking around) Who's cell phone is that? It's just so annoying; everywhere you go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think it's coming from your bag.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (checks) I never get calls!! (Answers the phone) Hello?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Hi, it's Eric. From the Halloween party, Ursula's fiancee.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God Eric hi! Wait, how'd you get this number?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh, I have a friend who's a cop and he got it for me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! What an incredible violation-and wonderful surprise.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Uh listen, I just-I thought you should know I broke up with Ursula.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh you did? (To Rachel) He did it! He did it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! What did he do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Shhh! I'm talking.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Anyway, I was wondering if, you were the sort of person who...eats lunch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you asking me out? 'Cause it would be kinda weird since you just broke up with my sister.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Yeah uh...okay. I'm-I'm sorry. Bye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Wait! I was just saying that so you'd think I was a good person. Fight for me.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Uhh, I won't take no for an answer.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not great, but we can work on it at lunch. Okay, I can be at your apartment in two hours.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Great! But wh-wh-How do you know where I live?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I've got friends too. Okay, bye.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Bye.
She hangs up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! I'm going out with Eric! Ooh, this day is really gonna be so much better than I thought it was gonna be. Oh Ross, I can't make lunch. (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So apparently I'm available for lunch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't. I'm busy. I'm apartment hunting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're moving?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I can't live with Joey once the baby comes. I don't want my child's first words to be, (in a baby's voice) "How you doin'?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So does-does Joey know you're moving?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I haven't discussed it with him yet, but I know he's gonna be relieved. Last week, he brought this girl over and I started talking to her about morning sickness and then I showed her pictures from my pregnancy book.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's not really porn.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Not so much.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y'know what and if you're looking for a place? I just heard in the elevator this morning that a woman in my building died.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Was she old? Does she have a view?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I don't know, but how-how great would that be huh? You living in my building. I could help take care of the baby. I can come over whenever I want. (Rachel looks at him.) With your permission.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah that would really be great.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well can we see it?! Oh maybe we shouldn't. I mean if she just died this morning out of respect.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. No. No you're right.
Pause as they both take another sip of coffee.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shall we?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.
They both exit.
Ross's Building, they are approaching the apartment of the woman who died. Ross knocks on the door and a woman answers it.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi. I'm Ross Geller. I live in the building.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And I'm Rachel, an admirer of the building.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I-I heard about Mrs. Verhoeven passing away and I'm so sorry for your loss.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: She didn't pass.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: My mother's still alive.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thank God!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: It looked like we were gonna lose her this morning, but she's a tough old bird.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ahh.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: Are you close with her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Of course! Uh yeah, she and I would talk all the time in-in (Rachel pokes her head in and starts to look around) the laundry room. (Pushes Rachel out of the way.)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: You speak Dutch? (In Dutch) Zeer vereerd een vriend van mijn moeder te ontmoeten. (Translation: I'm very honored to meet a friend of my mother.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know I would it's just painful.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So she's really not dead.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: No, she's hanging in there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmm. Do you think-Could you tell me if she's hanging in, in a one bedroom or a two?
Monica and Chandler's, Monica is observing the new maid, Brenda, clean.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: Mrs. Bing, this tile cleaner is incredible! Where'd you get it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh well umm, I make it myself! It's two parts ammonia and one part lemon juice. And now the secret ingredient is...y'know what? We just met.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: Okay. Uhh, I'm gonna go get the clothes from the laundry room now. And, when I come back I'll clean behind the refrigerator.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Chandler) I love her.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: I'll be back in a minute.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (As Brenda exits Monica notices something.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See? I told you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She stole my jeans!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pause) What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I have been looking for them all week and she is wearing them!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So she stole your pants and then she came back and wore them in front of you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't you see? It's the perfect crime!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She must've been planning this for years!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I will prove it to you! Okay? About a week ago I was wearing those jeans and I dropped a pen in my lap and it left an ink stain on the crotch. Now when she comes back I will find it and show you that stain!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, isn't it possible that the company that sold the jeans made more than just the one pair?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, shouldn't we go give her the benefit of the doubt before we go...snooping around her crotch?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine. I'm just glad I didn't give her my secret ingredient.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Out of curiosity, what is your secret ingredient?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! (Laughs.)
Eric's Apartment, he's opening the door to reveal Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Come in, I'm so glad you're here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, me too. Not in the shaky angry way you are though.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Sorry, I just saw Ursula. I had to give the engagement ring back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Just seeing her brought it all back. All the lies, the way she used me. I just...I got so angry just looking at her...(Looks at Phoebe)...face.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. (Covers her face with her hand.) Yeah.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I'm sorry. I just...when I look at you I see her. When I see her I get a little bit angry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe this is too weird.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: No wait! There's only a problem when I look at you. (Sits down on the couch.) Oh I got it! I got it. (Puts his hands to his eyes.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No don't tear out your eyes!!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I was just, I was just gonna take out my lenses.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah try that. (He finishes and looks at her.) So, is that better?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Not really. You...you're blurry, but you still look like Ursula. You're Blursula. Okay wait. Maybe...If I-if I just don't look at you for a while. (Stands up and turns his back to her.) See? It...it works. I'm not, I'm not angry at all anymore! This is a great date!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look Eric, turn around. (He does so.) Look, I like you, but it shouldn't be this hard. Y'know? This is our first date y'know? First dates are supposed to be about excitement and electricity and 'Ooh, he just touched my hand, did he mean to touch my hand?' and y'know first kisses and...(He kisses her)...second kisses. (Motions for him to kiss her again which he does and they start to make out.)
Central Perk, Ross is reading some book and Gunther serves him a cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks for the coffee, or bedankt voor de koffie, Gunter. (He translates that phrase into Dutch.)

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Jij spreekt Nederlands? Dat is te gek. Heb je familie daar? (Translation: You speak Dutch  That's cool.  Do you have relatives there?)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, we're done.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Ezel. (Translation: Donkey)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ezel? Ezel? Ezel? (Looks it up in his book.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey Ross! Listen, do you want to go see that new Imax movie on tide pools?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (laughs) No. But I got Knicks tickets for you, me, and Chandler.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sweet!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, well finish your coffee; let's go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay I-I just have to stop by my place first.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: To tape the game? You do this every time Ross, you're not gonna be on TV!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no, I-I have to see if this apartment became available.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, you're switching apartments?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's not for me, it's for Rachel.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But Rachel has an apartment.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, but when the baby comes she's gonna want to move.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, you didn't expect her to live there with a baby did you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I guess I didn't really think about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (finds the word in the book) Ezel! (Reads the translation.) Hey Gunther! You're an ezel!

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Jij hebt seks met ezels. (Translation: You have sex with donkeys.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Damnit!
Monica and Chandler's, Brenda is sweeping and Monica is sitting at the kitchen table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nice jeans!

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: Oh thanks! I like your top.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. (Holds on to it.) (To herself) You're not gettin' it.
Brenda bends down to use the dustpan and Monica leans over to look for the stain, but leans so far over she falls out of the chair.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: What happened?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I fell asleep.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: I was thinking about taking my lunch break.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, will you do the top of the cabinets? That'll really work up your appetite for lunch.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: All right.

Brenda pulls a chair to the counter and uses it to get up on the counter in order to clean the top of the cabinets. Monica sneaks over, bends over, and tries to see the stain. That doesn't work so she sticks her head between Brenda's legs. Suddenly Brenda changes her stance and traps Monica's head between her legs.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: What's going on?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry. I've never had a maid before, is this not okay?
Commercial Break
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is eating pizza as Joey returns from the Knicks game.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How was the game?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, okay. I...I ate way too much.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh. (Notices the pizza, grabs a slice, and takes a bite.) So umm, I was talkin' to Ross and he said you were looking for a new place.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah! Hopefully across the street if certain Dutch people would just let go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I was kinda hoping you'd stay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh but Joey, I have to go. There's no room for a baby here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No room? It's a baby. It's like this big. (Holds his hands about a foot apart.) Y'know, I mean you-you could you could put it over here. (A desk.) Or-or-or we could put it right here. (The chair.) Aw, it's cute, right? Or-or we could put it over here. (By the bathroom door.) You wouldn't even notice it. Where's the baby? (Mumbles that it's over in the corner.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, it's not just a matter of where you put it. I mean a baby changes everything. They cry all the time. I mean imagine bringing home some girl and trying to score when there's a screaming baby around.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I could use a challenge! It's getting pretty easy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, it's so sweet that you want me to stay, but I-I can't do that to you. I mean it would disrupt your entire life.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I love living with you so much. I just wish things didn't have to change.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y'know I blame Ross for this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I do too a little bit.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm gonna miss you, you're the hottest roommate I ever had.
Eric's Apartment, he and Phoebe are still making out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, oh no! I have to go! I have a massage appointment.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh no, stay here we'll keep doing this. I'll pay you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I got in trouble for that before. I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Absolutely. (They kiss and Phoebe heads for the door.) I love the way you kiss.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really? That's the thing I'm worse at! You'll see. (Exits.)
Monica and Chandler's, Brenda is shaking out the rugs on the balcony as Monica pouts in the living room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! Umm, I think Brenda needs a raise.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because I put my head between her legs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To see her pants?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They're my pants!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you sure? Did you see the stain?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! I was just getting into position and then everything went dark.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: God! She is not stealing from us! Okay, will you let this go?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine. (Brenda comes in to use the bathroom and adjusts her pink bra strap on the way.) She's wearing my bra!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh dear God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My pink flowered bra! I recognize the strap!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And yet you don't recognize that you're crazy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here's the plan! Okay? I'm going to leave you get a look at Brenda's bra!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here's another plan...No!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I would do it but she thinks I'm attracted to her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you not hear where my head was? Come on! Come on we're a team! We're in this together!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I fear a jury will see it the same way!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do this for me! Come on, I catch you looking at woman's breasts all the time!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You see that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you see this? (Mimics him drooling over a woman's breasts.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right. Yes. Okay. I get your point. But if it's not your bra will you just let the woman clean the apartment?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! Absolutely. Okay? Look, you'll know it's mine because on the right cup, the lacey part, there's a very noticeable rip.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You need new clothes.
Mrs. Verhoeven's Apartment, Ross is back to inquire about the elder Verhoeven's health or lack there of.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi. How is she?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: It's not looking good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (happily) Oh. (Realizes then sadly) Oh. Well I uh, I brought her some bloemen. (Flowers in Dutch.)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: That's so sweet. (Takes the flowers.) Would you like to come in and say good-bye? I'm sure it would mean a lot to her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh I don't know that it would.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: Well, her memory is pretty much gone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right then. (Follows her in and checks the place out.)
Eric's Apartment, he's resting on the bed as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Welcome back!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! (Jumps on the bed with him.) Can we pick up where we left off?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I don't know, I'm still pretty tired out from this afternoon.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Uh, the sex.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What sex?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Our sex.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We didn't have sex.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Well if I didn't have sex with you, I had sex with someone that looked an awful lot like...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (simultaneously as Eric) Ew! Ew! Ew! Ew! Ew! Ew!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: (simultaneously as Phoebe) Oh no! Oh no! Oh no! Oh no! No! No! No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You-you...you had sex with Ursula?!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Uh, a little bit. She-she-she walked in and I thought she was you and I kissed her and...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You didn't notice she was wearing different clothes?!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Well I was just so excited to see you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Ew! Ew! Ew! Ugh! Y'know what? This is too weird.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: No-no it's not! I don't want to lose you! It's-it's like I was saying to Ursula when I was making love to her and I thought she was you-Yeah it is too weird.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So I guess this is it.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Yeah. (They hug.) Maybe it's for the best. You smell just like her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, so do you.
Monica and Chandler's, Brenda is wiping the coffee table and Chandler is trying to look at her bra and leans over on the coffee table to get a good look.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: (noticing him) What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm leaning. This is where I lean.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: Okay. (Goes over and fluffs up the pillows on the couch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Brenda a bee!

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! It's flown into your blouse and you'd better undo your buttons lest it sting you!

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: I think I know what's going on here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: Look, I know it must be hard that your wife is a lesbian, but it's wrong. You're married.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I totally understand. (They both laugh.) Can I just see your bra?
The Hallway, Monica is sitting on the step as Rachel returns.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (looking at Rachel's jeans) Where did you get those jeans?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You gave them to me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No I didn't!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, I took them. But I figured it would be okay because you got a big ink stain on the crotch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no! Did you take my bra too?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What bra?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The pink one with the flowers?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You mean the one that you're wearing? (Adjusts Monica's pink bra strap as Monica looks down her shirt.)

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: (entering) I quit! (Storms off.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sounds about right.
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel enters and notices that Joey has set up a space for the baby where the couch was, complete with a crib.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What is this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Uh, this is just to give you an idea. Okay well, we can put screens here, (In front of the crib.) so that the baby has privacy, and-and-and maybe a mobile over the crib. And uh-Oh look! Here's a baby monitor (Holds it up), which until the baby comes we can use as walkie-talkies. Huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're so sweet. (Notices something in the crib.) Oh my God! And you gave the baby Hugsy! (A stuffed penguin wearing a ski jacket, goggles, and hat.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhhhhhh.... That-that-that's really just to show where the baby would go. Y'know why don't I hold on to him so that there's no confusion? (Takes him back, sets him on the chair, and apologizes to him.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But Joey the baby is going to be crying, it's going to be loud.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm loud!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's gonna be up all night!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm up all night!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's gonna poop!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What about all the women you want to bring home?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, if I'm bringing home a woman who can't stand being around a baby, then maybe I don't want to be with that woman! Or maybe we'll just do it in the bathroom of the club!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, are you sure?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! All right-Look, I know sometimes it'll be hard, okay? But, it'll also be really...really great. Please Rachel! I-I-I really want you to stay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I want me to stay too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. (They hug.) Oh Joey and look at this crib! It's so cute!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know! I found it on the street.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you serious-Really?! It's in such good condition.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! Whoa-whoa what's under the covers?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's moving.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ew.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's still-(Screams)-It's got a tail! Get it out of here! Get it out of here!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh! Ah! Okay! (Quickly drags the crib outside.)
Dedicated to the Memory of Pearl Harmon
Closing Credits
Joey and Rachel's, Joey and Rachel are toasting her staying put.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, the old lady died. And how do I know? Her dying wish was for one last kiss. But I don't care, (To Rachel) because you got the apartment. Yes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ewww. Yeah. Umm. I think I'm gonna stay here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Isn't that great?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stutters looking for words) Ezels!!
End
